key_finding: Wisconsin's highest-poverty counties (Rusk, Forest at 80.0%) would benefit most from this food access policy (statewide mean poverty rate: 10.5%).
Counties with the highest poverty rates (Menominee, Milwaukee at 21.2%) face the greatest childcare affordability burden (statewide mean poverty rate: 10.5%).
The lowest-income counties (Langlade, Ashland) face the highest relative property tax burden for school funding, making them the primary beneficiaries of income-to-property tax reform.
Counties where racial and economic disadvantage compound — particularly Milwaukee, Racine — show the greatest need for equitable education policy.
The lowest-income counties (Langlade, Ashland) face the highest relative property tax burden for school funding, making them the primary beneficiaries of income-to-property tax reform.
Lower-wage counties like Langlade, Ashland have the most to gain from stronger labor protections, collective bargaining, and wage standards.
The lowest-income counties in Wisconsin (Langlade, Ashland) would see the greatest relative benefit from a $20 minimum wage indexed to cost of living.
